head.hi: Mr. Myron C. Taylor, Personal Representative of President Roosevelt to Pope Pius XII, to the President
head.note: Copy transmitted to the Secretary of State with the following memorandum of September 22, 1944, from President Roosevelt: “For preparation of reply for my signature.”

div.p: Dear Mr. President: I recently wired youTelegram 301, September 6, 10 a.m., not printed. that His Holiness had requested more detailed information than I had as yet seen fit to give to him in respect to the International Organization for the Preservation of Peace.For correspondence on this subject, see pp. 614 ff. In my wire I enquire to what extent Secretary Hull and yourself wished me to go in explaining the details of the plan which I followed in the consultationsIn April and May of 1944 Secretary of State Hull authorized Mr. Taylor, who was a member of the Department of State Informal Political Agenda Group concerned with a “Possible Plan for the Establishment of an International Organization for the Maintenance of International Peace and Security”, to hold consultations with the three jurists mentioned. See Postwar Foreign Policy Preparation, 1939–1945 (Washington, Government Printing Office, 1949), pp. 169–173, 247–255 and 263–265. which I had with Chief Justice Hughes,Charles Evans Hughes, Chief Justice of the United States, 1930–1941. Honorable John W. DavisDemocratic presidential candidate in 1924. and Honorable Nathan L. Miller.Governor of New York, 1921–1923.
I now have a similar written request from His Holiness, of which I am attaching a copy. Your advice in the premises will be very greatly appreciated and with best regards, believe me.

frus:attachment.head: The Papal Under Secretary of State (Montini) to Mr. Myron C. Taylor, Personal Representative of President Roosevelt to Pope Pius XII
frus:attachment.p:
Your Excellency: His Holiness has graciously indicated to me that in your Audience of August 30th you made reference to an
International Organization for the Preservation of Peace, which is the object of serious study of President Roosevelt and his advisers.

Since the Holy See, by the very nature of its high mission, is deeply interested in any project which might further the cause of lasting peace, I should be indeed grateful if you were to find it possible to provide me with further details regarding the nature of the proposed organization and the plans for its activation and development.
